title: nexus hexa-sim CLI subcommand integrity audit (Ω-cycle)
task: audit dispatch ↔ help ↔ tool-existence ↔ executability ↔ smoke for all hexa-sim subcommands

inventory.method: regex 'sub == "X"' inside cmd_hexa_sim() body of cli/run.hexa

matrix.schema: subcommand | dispatch_path | exists | executable | in_help | smoke
matrix.notes: .hexa scripts intentionally not chmod +x — invoked via 'hexa run', not directly

deferred_for_user_review.scope: tool/hexa_sim_falsifier.hexa selftest body — not dispatch (raw 71: not obvious bug, may indicate registry path drift)
tool/hexa_sim_ci.hexa — 2 of 19 tools failing selftest; likely correlated with falsifier selftest bug above

smoke.method: for each sub: invoke 'nexus hexa-sim <sub> --help' (or --selftest / --list / --text for those without --help) and verify exit=0 + sane first-line output
smoke.tool_runtime_failures_classification: pre-existing tool-level bugs surfaced by smoke; dispatch path itself works

readme_cross_check.rationale: README is a corpus index (witnesses + tools by file path), not a CLI subcommand reference. Per-subcommand listing is owned by _hexa_sim_help in cli/run.hexa. No drift to fix.

fixpoint.claim: Every dispatched subcommand resolves to an existing tool (.sh chmod +x or .hexa via 'hexa run'); help text strings synced to actual dispatch table; no orphan dispatches; no missing dispatches.
fixpoint.reproducer: cd /Users/ghost/core/nexus && python3 -c 'import re; c=open("cli/run.hexa").read(); ...' (see audit method in axes.inventory)
fixpoint.next_review_trigger: next time _hexa_sim_help text count strings change OR new bridge added
